text: The pipeline shall have exactly two stages: generate and finalize, each invoked by explicit user commands
The generate stage shall read a dataset configuration file or frozen specification and produce HDF5 shards in a staging area
The finalize stage shall read validated staged shards and produce train/val/test splits in the specified output format (HDF5 or WebDataset) plus normalization statistics and dataset.complete marker
The pipeline shall support resumable generation where re-running generate regenerates only missing or invalid shards
Cloud credentials (R2, RunPod API key) shall be validated before launching any worker instances
Local execution mode shall be supported using Docker containers and local filesystem instead of cloud infrastructure
Cloudflare R2 shall serve as the authoritative source of truth for shard completeness, determined by file existence and validation markers
Shard identifiers shall be logical and deterministic (shard-000000 through shard-N), independent of which worker instance produces them
The pipeline shall determine remaining work through reconciliation by comparing the input specification against validated shards present in storage
Each pipeline stage shall be independently executable and capable of determining its own completion without communicating with other stages
Worker processes shall write artifacts only under the metadata/workers/ prefix; the finalize process shall write canonical outputs only under the data/ prefix
Worker processes shall never write directly to canonical shard paths (data/shards/)
Each shard shall render in a separate operating system process via multiprocessing with spawn context to isolate renderer crashes
Debug logs shall be uploaded to R2 via bash EXIT trap even if the worker process terminates unexpectedly
All rclone file transfers shall use the --checksum flag to verify transfer integrity
Worker-side shard validation shall include minimum three checks: valid HDF5 file format, expected datasets present (audio, mel_spec, param_array), row count matches specification
The design target for shard validation includes four checks, adding shape validation and value bounds checking to the minimum three checks
Finalize shall perform structural validation on staged shards before promoting them to canonical paths
When multiple valid staged shard attempts exist for the same shard ID, finalize shall select the lexicographically smallest filename for promotion
The .valid lifecycle marker shall be written only as the final step after rendering, local validation, upload, and bookkeeping are complete
Corrupt shards shall be uploaded to quarantine subdirectories and marked with .invalid markers for preservation and debugging
Generation throughput shall support at least 500,000 samples in under 2 hours with 10 parallel workers
100% of shard generation failures shall include actionable error messages describing the failure cause
The pipeline shall support generation and finalization of multi-terabyte datasets
The cost per 500,000-sample dataset generation run shall not exceed $5 USD
The input specification shall be immutable after initial creation and shall not be modified during or after execution
The input specification shall include: git commit SHA, is_repo_dirty boolean flag, renderer version, and per-shard seeds for reproducibility
All machine-generated structured files (specifications, reports, dataset cards) shall use JSON format
User-authored configuration files shall use YAML format
Worker debug logs shall use JSONL (JSON Lines) format for append-only streaming
Dataset cards (dataset.json) shall be self-describing and comprehensible without access to the metadata directory
The pipeline shall write the dataset.complete marker as the final step after all outputs are uploaded and verified
The pipeline shall be safe to execute at any time, in any order, and multiple times without risk of data corruption
The pipeline shall support two output formats: HDF5 (for local random-access training) and WebDataset (for distributed streaming training)
WebDataset .tar archives shall contain samples as individual NumPy files using the naming pattern {sample_id}.audio.npy, {sample_id}.params.npy, and {sample_id}.mel.npy
The pipeline shall not use consensus protocols, distributed transactions, service discovery, or message queues for coordination
The pipeline shall not implement automatic stage chaining; stage transitions shall be explicit user-invoked commands
The pipeline shall not poll cloud provider job status APIs; work completeness shall be determined from storage state only
The pipeline shall not include speculative provider abstractions; only RunPod and local compute backends shall be implemented
Worker reports shall include content hashes (SHA-256), per-shard results (success/failure, row count, render time), and error details for all attempts
Worker environment information (CPU architecture, OS information) shall be recorded to detect floating-point non-determinism across hardware
Cloudflare R2 shall provide strong read-after-write consistency for PUT and LIST operations
Concurrent execution of generate or finalize on the same run shall not corrupt data; duplicate work is the only consequence
Worker uploads after finalize completion shall not affect canonical finalized data due to staging/canonical prefix separation
Content hashes shall be used to detect and surface floating-point non-determinism across different CPU architectures
The output format (HDF5 or WebDataset) shall be a configuration choice specified at run creation and frozen in the input specification
Training/validation/test split ratios shall be configurable and recorded immutably in the input specification
All configuration schemas shall be validated using Pydantic with strict=True mode to catch type errors at parse time
Shard generation shall be deterministic within the same Docker image and hardware architecture using the same input specification
Per-shard random number generator seeds shall be computed deterministically as base_seed + shard_id
Worker lifecycle markers (.rendering, .valid, .invalid) shall be written as empty files to signal attempt state transitions
Each worker attempt shall use a unique identifier in file names to prevent overwrites of previous attempts
The make status command shall display reconciliation results by analyzing storage without querying cloud provider APIs
Local compute mode (LocalBackend) shall use identical spec format and validation logic as cloud mode for complete behavioral fidelity
W&B artifacts shall be named data-{dataset_config_id} of type 'dataset' with metadata including shard count, provenance, and splits
W&B artifacts shall reference the R2 path to actual data without uploading the raw shard data to W&B
Pipeline execution metrics shall be recorded in wandb.summary including shard counts (total, valid, quarantined), total samples, generation time, and finalize time
The pipeline shall detect and report the existence and count of quarantined shards in status reports
The evaluation pipeline must run on local macOS machines, local Linux machines, Docker containers, and CI runners
Environment differences must be handled by configuration, not by code forks
Every link in the debuggability chain (CSV → per-sample CSV → rendered audio → predicted parameters → checkpoint → training run → dataset) must be inspectable
Every make target must be safe to re-run without data loss or side effects
All rclone operations must use the --checksum flag
Fresh clone must complete eval pipeline to metrics CSV in under 15 minutes on a small fixture
No hardcoded /data/scratch/ paths may appear in committed configuration files
The 19 SGE scripts in jobs/predict/ must remain unchanged and deprecated
No new code may reference SGE (Sun Grid Engine) directives
The make eval target must run predict → render → metrics in sequence
The make predict target must auto-download checkpoints from W&B when referenced via ${wandb:...} resolver
The make render target must accept PRED_DIR and OUTPUT_DIR parameters
The make metrics target must accept AUDIO_DIR and OUTPUT_DIR parameters
The make upload-eval target must sync eval artifacts to R2 using rclone with checksums
The make docker-eval target must run the full eval pipeline inside a Docker container
Predict, render, and metrics must be explicit make targets, not automatically chained
The system must not use a DAG orchestrator (Airflow, Prefect, or custom DAG engine)
The predict stage must load a trained checkpoint via PyTorch Lightning's Trainer.predict()
The predict stage must run inference on the test dataset split
The predict stage must output predicted parameter tensors via a PredictionWriter callback
The predict stage must resolve dataset_root from data.dataset_root configuration
When data.r2_path is explicitly set, SurgeDataModule.prepare_data() must sync from R2 before loading
Checkpoint paths must support ${wandb:...} OmegaConf resolver syntax for W&B artifact download
The predict output directory must be ${paths.output_dir}/predictions
The render stage must load predicted parameter tensors from disk
The render stage must render audio via the Surge XT VST plugin using pedalboard library
The render stage must output both predicted and ground-truth target audio waveforms
The render stage must auto-detect display availability (Xvfb on Linux, native on macOS)
The render stage must default plugin path to plugins/Surge XT.vst3
The render stage must default preset path to presets/surge-base.vstpreset
The metrics stage must compute MSS, wMFCC, SOT, and RMS metrics
The metrics stage must parallelize metric computation using ProcessPoolExecutor
The metrics stage must output metrics.csv (per-sample) and aggregated_metrics.csv
The r2_path configuration must have no default value - R2 download must be explicit opt-in only
If dataset_root already contains data matching checksum, no R2 download must occur
Checkpoints must be stored in W&B artifacts, not in R2
The ${wandb:...} OmegaConf resolver must resolve lazily on first access of ckpt_path
The ${wandb:...} resolver must check .cache/checkpoints/ for cached copies before calling W&B API
The ${wandb:...} resolver must download from W&B artifacts using wandb.Api().artifact().download()
Checkpoint resolution must follow Hydra's standard override precedence: CLI > Experiment config > Base config
Checkpoint resolution must support three patterns: CLI argument, experiment config with pinned W&B artifact, and null (training only)
The base eval.yaml must require ckpt_path to force explicit user specification
Eval artifact paths in R2 must follow the pattern: eval/{dataset_config_id}/{dataset_wandb_run_id}/{train_config_id}/{train_wandb_run_id}/{eval_config_id}/{eval_wandb_run_id}/
W&B eval runs must declare input artifacts (model checkpoint + dataset) using use_artifact()
W&B eval runs must log summary metrics (MSS, wMFCC, SOT, RMS)
W&B eval runs must have job_type set to 'evaluation'
configs/data/surge_simple.yaml must replace hardcoded paths with ${paths.data_dir}/{dataset_config_id}/{dataset_wandb_run_id} pattern
configs/data/surge_mini.yaml must replace hardcoded paths with ${paths.data_dir}/{dataset_config_id}/{dataset_wandb_run_id} pattern
.env.example must contain only R2 credentials and WANDB_API_KEY, no path variables
The make predict target must fail with a clear error message if dataset is not found
On headless Linux, Xvfb must be auto-launched with DISPLAY exported before rendering
On headless Linux, Xvfb must be cleaned up after rendering completes
On macOS, rendering must use the native display without launching Xvfb
The checkpoint cache directory must be $PROJECT_ROOT/.cache/checkpoints/
The W&B resolver must not be called multiple times for the same artifact in a single run
Hydra config files must be frozen and stored with eval outputs for reproducibility
W&B must store training metrics, checkpoints (via log_model: all), and eval summary metrics
R2 must store generated datasets and eval bulk artifacts (predictions, audio, metrics)
Local logs directory must store Hydra output, TensorBoard logs, and temporary checkpoints
The system must register the ${wandb:...} resolver in src/utils/utils.py via register_resolvers()
The {{wandb:...}} resolver implementation must use wandb.Api() without requiring an active W&B run context
Experiment configs must pin model variants with stable W&B artifact references
Dataset download in prepare_data() must run before data loaders are created
The W&B Teams plan must be used for checkpoint storage to support unlimited tracking hours
dataset_config_id must be constructed from the config filename stem in configs/dataset/{id}.yaml
dataset_wandb_run_id must be configurable via wandb.init(id=...) with default format {dataset_config_id}-{YYYYMMDDTHHMMSSZ}
train_config_id must be constructed from the config filename stem in configs/experiment/.../{id}.yaml
train_wandb_run_id must be configurable via wandb.init(id=...) with default format {train_config_id}-{YYYYMMDDTHHMMSSZ}
eval_config_id must be constructed from the eval dataset config filename stem in configs/dataset/{id}.yaml
eval_wandb_run_id must be configurable via wandb.init(id=...) with default format {eval_config_id}-{YYYYMMDDTHHMMSSZ}
W&B run ID must not exceed 64 characters
R2 bucket intermediate-data/ must contain subdirectories data/, train/, and eval/ following the specified path structure
Workers must only write under metadata/workers/ directory in data generation R2 paths
shards/ directory must be written only by the finalize process
Datasets become immutable once dataset.complete file exists
New dataset versions must use a new dataset_wandb_run_id
Training must set log_model='all' in the W&B Lightning Logger to persist checkpoints as W&B artifacts
Dataset artifact name must follow pattern data-{dataset_config_id}
Model artifact name must follow pattern model-{train_config_id}
Eval-results artifact name must follow pattern eval-{eval_config_id}
W&B must auto-version artifacts with :v0, :v1, :v2 suffixes on subsequent runs of the same config
W&B must automatically set :latest alias on every log_artifact call
Training script must set :best alias on model artifact when validation metric improves using run.log_artifact(art, aliases=['best'])
Promote workflow must set :production alias on model artifacts when promoting to GitHub Release
Every run must call run.use_artifact() for all input artifacts
Every run must call run.log_artifact() for all output artifacts
Runs must use run.use_artifact() instead of api.artifact() to create lineage links
Every run must include github_sha in wandb.config
wandb.config must contain hyperparameters that are set before the run
wandb.summary must contain final metrics that are measured after the run
artifact.metadata must contain properties of the artifact itself
Dataset properties must be logged to the dataset artifact, not to training runs that consume it
Data pipeline stage must use job_type='data-generation' in wandb.init()
Training stage must use job_type='training' in wandb.init()
Evaluation stage must use job_type='evaluation' in wandb.init()
Every wandb.init() call must set the job_type parameter
Tests workflow must trigger on push and PR events
Tests workflow must run on ubuntu-latest and macos-latest runners
Full Tests workflow must trigger on schedule and dispatch events
Full Tests workflow must run on gpu-x64 runner
Data Generation workflow must use workflow_call trigger type
Data Generation workflow must run on ubuntu-latest-4core runner
All workflows that create W&B runs must export GITHUB_SHA to the run environment
Evaluation workflow must accept train_wandb_run_id as an input parameter
Evaluation workflow must accept eval_config_id as an input parameter
Promotion workflow must accept train_wandb_run_id as an input parameter
Promotion workflow must accept eval_wandb_run_id as an input parameter
GitHub Release tags must follow format model-v{N} where N is an incrementing integer
Secrets must only be available to workflows that require them
Training workflows must have R2 read access for downloading dataset shards
Training workflows must have R2 write access for uploading checkpoints
W&B Entity must be set to tinaudio
W&B Project must be set to synth-setter
Configs must use environment variable syntax entity: ${oc.env:WANDB_ENTITY,tinaudio}
Configs must use environment variable syntax project: ${oc.env:WANDB_PROJECT,synth-setter}
W&B artifacts must reference R2 objects via artifact.add_reference('s3://intermediate-data/...')
Environments calling add_reference() must have AWS_ENDPOINT_URL or WANDB_S3_ENDPOINT_URL set to the R2 endpoint
Environments downloading reference artifacts must have AWS_ENDPOINT_URL or WANDB_S3_ENDPOINT_URL set to the R2 endpoint
Artifacts must not duplicate large data files
Bulk data must live in R2, not stored within W&B artifacts
*_wandb_run_id must uniquely identify an immutable run output
Runs must not consume data from R2 paths that lack a completion marker
R2 paths must be append-only after completion markers exist
All configs must be frozen into artifacts or R2 storage paths at run time
Training must run successfully on local Linux machines
Training must run successfully on local macOS machines
Training must run successfully in Docker
Training must run successfully on RunPod
Checkpoints must survive pod death
Resume from last durable checkpoint must restore full optimizer state
Resume from last durable checkpoint must restore full scheduler state
Dataset version must be inspectable from training run metadata
Training configuration must be inspectable from training run metadata
Git SHA must be inspectable from training run metadata
Checkpoint lineage must be inspectable from training run metadata
Training must execute as a single long-running job (not distributed)
Training must support launch via `make train EXPERIMENT=<experiment_name>`
Training must support launch via `make runpod-train EXPERIMENT=<experiment_name>`
Training must support launch via `make docker-train EXPERIMENT=<experiment_name>`
Resume must support local checkpoint paths via `ckpt_path=<local_path>`
Resume must support W&B artifact references via `ckpt_path=wandb:model-<name>:<alias>`
W&B logging must set job_type to 'training'
W&B logging must call run.use_artifact() for the dataset artifact
W&B logging must call run.log_artifact() for the model artifact
W&B config must include github_sha
Model artifacts must be named according to pattern model-{train_config_id}
Dataset artifacts must be named according to pattern data-{dataset_config_id}
Every checkpoint (last, best, intermediate) must be uploaded to W&B immediately after save
Checkpoint durability must be achieved through WandbLogger with log_model='all'
Pod death must result in loss of at most one checkpoint interval
RunPod launcher must be implemented as a thin launcher script (not a backend abstraction)
Training must use a separate Docker image from the data pipeline
W&B artifact downloads must be cached locally during resume
Promotion must remain a separate workflow (not automatic after training)
Same training experiment configuration must produce consistent behavior across local, Docker, and RunPod environments
Dataset access must follow storage provenance convention
The entrypoint must dispatch on the MODE environment variable.
The entrypoint must exit with code 1 if MODE is unset or empty.
The entrypoint must exit with code 1 if MODE has an unknown value.
When MODE=idle, the entrypoint must execute 'sleep infinity'.
When MODE=passthrough with arguments, the entrypoint must execute the provided arguments as a command.
When MODE=passthrough with no arguments, the entrypoint must exit with code 0.
When MODE=generate_dataset, the entrypoint must run VST dataset generation via 'pipeline.entrypoints.generate_dataset' under headless X11.
The generate_dataset mode must accept configuration via environment variables instead of CLI arguments.
The Dockerfile must define a 'dev-snapshot' target.
The 'dev-snapshot' target must use 'docker_entrypoint.sh' as the entrypoint.
The 'dev-snapshot' target must inherit from 'r2-config-base'.
R2 credentials must be baked into the image only when BuildKit secrets are provided at build time.
A placeholder rclone config must be used when R2 BuildKit secrets are not provided.
W&B authentication must be baked into '~/.netrc' when the 'wandb_api_key' BuildKit secret is provided at build time.
When the 'wandb_api_key' BuildKit secret is not provided, the WANDB_API_KEY environment variable must be required at runtime.
The IMAGE build argument must default to 'dev-snapshot'.
The SYNTH_PERMUTATIONS_GIT_REF build argument must default to 'main'.
The SURGE_GIT_REF build argument must default to a pinned SHA.
The BUILD_MODE build argument must default to 'source' and accept values 'source' or 'prebuilt'.
The R2_BUCKET build argument must default to empty.
The TORCH_INDEX_URL build argument must be required.
The image must set SYNTH_PERMUTATIONS_GIT_REF to the git reference value used at build time.
The image must set R2_BUCKET from the build argument value.
The image must set VIRTUAL_ENV to '/venv/main'.
The image must set PATH to '$VIRTUAL_ENV/bin:$PATH'.
The DATASET_CONFIG environment variable must be required for MODE=generate_dataset.
The RUN_METADATA_DIR environment variable must default to '/run-metadata' for MODE=generate_dataset.
When MODE=generate_dataset, the container must materialize a DatasetPipelineSpec.
When MODE=generate_dataset, the container must upload the spec and shard to R2.
When MODE=generate_dataset, the container must write input_spec.json to RUN_METADATA_DIR.
When MODE=generate_dataset, the container must generate samples equal to shard_size.
When MODE=generate_dataset with num_shards > 1, the container must raise NotImplementedError.
